basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: - Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/eDCRule/ReleaseNotes.md) - There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The eDCRule solution for Microsoft Sentinel installs custom analytic rule templates. **Analytic Rules:** 10 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

metadata.hidden: Hiding location, we get it from the log analytics workspace

basics.name: getLAWorkspace
basics.toolTip: This filters by workspaces that exist in the Resource Group selected

basics.name: workspace
basics.label: Workspace
basics.toolTip: This dropdown will list only workspace that exists in the Resource Group selected

options.text: This solution installs analytic rule templates. After installing the solution, create and enable analytic rules in the Manage solution view.

elements.name: analytic1
elements.label: [AzureSubscription] Suspicious Azure VM Run Command Execution Detected

options.text: Detects Azure VM Run Command execution correlated with unusual sign-in behavior from UEBA. This can indicate administrative abuse or post-compromise activity.

elements.name: analytic2
elements.label: [Entra ID] Application Assigned Administrator Permissions Immediately After Obtaining Role Management Permissions

options.text: Detects an application receiving administrator permissions and then using them to assign a role. This pattern can indicate rapid privilege escalation through app consent abuse.

elements.name: analytic3
elements.label: [Entra ID] Application Granted Administrative Permission to Assign Microsoft Entra ID Roles

options.text: Detects when an application receives permission to assign Microsoft Entra ID roles. This can enable directory privilege escalation and should be reviewed immediately.

elements.name: analytic4
elements.label: [Entra ID] Authentication Method Changed for Privileged Account

options.text: Detects changes to authentication methods on privileged accounts. This may indicate an attacker added a new method to maintain access.

elements.name: analytic5
elements.label: [Entra ID] Domain Federation Trust Settings Modified

options.text: Detects changes to domain federation trust settings or domain authentication mode. These changes can be used to redirect sign-in trust and should be reviewed immediately.

elements.name: analytic6
elements.label: [Entra ID] Mass Privileged Role Change Activity Detected

options.text: Detects multiple privileged role changes occurring around the same time. This may signal bulk abuse or administrative misconfiguration.
Detects denied privilege elevation requests in Microsoft Entra ID. Review the requester and the target workflow for possible abuse or misconfiguration.

elements.name: analytic8
elements.label: [Entra ID] Privileged Role Assigned to a New User

options.text: Detects when a privileged role is assigned to a new user account. This can indicate unauthorized elevation of privileges.

elements.name: analytic9
elements.label: [Entra ID] Privileged Role Assigned to User

options.text: Detects when a user is assigned a privileged role in Microsoft Entra ID. This may indicate unauthorized privilege escalation and should be validated promptly.

elements.name: analytic10
elements.label: [Entra ID] Suspicious Continuous OAuth Token Usage

options.text: Detects repeated use of the same OAuth token across different IPs or locations over time. This can indicate token theft or session abuse.
